name: The Norns
description: The Norns are female beings in Norse mythology who control the destiny of gods and men. They are often described as giantesses or dwarves, and their origins are shrouded in mystery. The most prominent Norns are Urðr (Wyrd), Verðandi, and Skuld, representing the past, present, and future, respectively. They dwell at the Well of Urðr (Well of Wyrd) at the roots of Yggdrasil, the world tree, where they carve runes that determine the fate of all beings. Their decisions are immutable, even by the gods themselves. The Norns are not necessarily benevolent; their pronouncements can be harsh and unforgiving. They are responsible for both good fortune and misfortune, life and death. While the gods may influence events, the Norns ultimately hold the reins of fate. They water the roots of Yggdrasil with water and clay to keep the tree alive, signifying their role in maintaining the cosmos. They represent the inevitable flow of time and the interconnectedness of all things. Their power is absolute, making them among the most powerful entities in Norse cosmology. They are figures of profound respect and, at times, fear, representing the ultimate forces shaping existence.
subtitle: Weavers of Fate and Destiny

keyMyths.title: The Norns at Yggdrasil
keyMyths.description: The Norns reside at the Well of Urðr, tending to Yggdrasil and carving runes that dictate the fates of gods and men. They represent the past, present, and future, constantly shaping the tapestry of existence.

keyMyths.title: The Fate of Baldr
keyMyths.description: The Norns foretold the death of Baldr, a prophecy that ultimately came to pass despite the efforts of the gods to prevent it. This illustrates the Norns' unyielding power over destiny.

keyMyths.title: The Final Fate of the Gods
keyMyths.description: The Norns know the ultimate fate of the gods and the events of Ragnarok, the end of the world. Their knowledge underscores their profound understanding of the flow of time and the inevitable cycle of creation and destruction.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source for Norse mythology, including information about the Norns and their role in shaping destiny.
sources.text: A key source for Norse mythology, including information about the Norns and their role in shaping destiny.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems that provide insights into Norse beliefs and mythology, including references to the Norns.
